# BizUp Pay — LLM Integration Guide

> One TypeScript SDK for every Israeli payment provider. Write payment code once, swap providers by config.

Docs: https://pay.bizup.dev
GitHub: https://github.com/bizup-dev/bizup-pay
Full reference: https://pay.bizup.dev/llms-full.txt

## Install

Pick a provider and install core + adapter:

```bash
npm install @bizup-pay/core @bizup-pay/cardcom    # or morning, icount, grow
```

For browser payment UI (iframe/modal/redirect):

```bash
npm install @bizup-pay/client
```

## Providers

| Provider | Package | Config keys |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| Cardcom | `@bizup-pay/cardcom` | `terminalNumber`, `apiName`, `apiPassword` |
| Morning (Green Invoice) | `@bizup-pay/morning` | `apiKey`, `apiSecret` |
| iCount | `@bizup-pay/icount` | `companyId`, `user`, `pass` |
| Grow.il (Meshulam) | `@bizup-pay/grow` | `pageCode`, `apiKey` |

## Server-Side: Create a Payment Session

```typescript
import { createProvider } from '@bizup-pay/core'
import '@bizup-pay/cardcom' // registers the provider — swap this import to switch

const provider = createProvider('cardcom', {
  terminalNumber: 1000,
  apiName: process.env.CARDCOM_API_NAME!,
  apiPassword: process.env.CARDCOM_API_PASSWORD!,
})

const session = await provider.createSession({
  amount: 100,
  currency: 'ILS',
  description: 'Order #1234',
  successUrl: 'https://myshop.co.il/success',
  webhookUrl: 'https://myshop.co.il/api/webhook',
  customer: { name: 'Israel Israeli', email: 'israel@example.com' },
})

// session.pageUrl — redirect or mount this URL for the customer to pay
```

## Server-Side: Handle Webhook

```typescript
// POST /api/webhook
const event = await provider.parseWebhook(req.body)

if (event.type === 'payment.completed') {
  // event.transaction has: id, amount, status, paymentMethod, cardBrand, cardLastFour, documentUrl
  await fulfillOrder(event.transaction)
}
// Other types: 'payment.failed', 'payment.cancelled'
```

## Server-Side: Get Transaction & Refund

```typescript
const tx = await provider.getTransaction('tx_123')
// tx.amount, tx.status, tx.cardBrand, tx.documentUrl, tx.customer

const refund = await provider.refund({ transactionId: 'tx_123' })
// refund.id, refund.amount, refund.status
```

## Browser: Mount Payment UI

```typescript
import { BizupPay } from '@bizup-pay/client'

const bizupPay = new BizupPay()
bizupPay.mount(session, document.getElementById('payment'), {
  onSuccess: () => window.location.href = '/thank-you',
  onFailure: (err) => console.error(err),
  onCancel: () => console.log('cancelled'),
})
```

Modes: `iframe` (embed in container), `modal` (overlay), `redirect` (full page navigation).

## Switch Providers

Change the import and config — all other code stays identical:

```typescript
// Before: Cardcom
import '@bizup-pay/cardcom'
const provider = createProvider('cardcom', { terminalNumber: 1000, apiName: '...', apiPassword: '...' })

// After: Morning
import '@bizup-pay/morning'
const provider = createProvider('morning', { apiKey: '...', apiSecret: '...' })
```

## Error Handling

All errors are `BizupPayError` with structured fields:

```typescript
try {
  await provider.createSession(params)
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof BizupPayError) {
    err.code      // 'AUTHENTICATION_ERROR' | 'VALIDATION_ERROR' | 'PROVIDER_ERROR' | 'NETWORK_ERROR'
    err.provider  // 'cardcom' | 'morning' | 'icount' | 'grow'
    err.message   // human-readable
    err.providerError // raw error from provider API
  }
}
```

## Testing with Mock Server

```bash
npm install --save-dev @bizup-pay/mock-server
```

```typescript
import { createMockServer } from '@bizup-pay/mock-server'

const mock = createMockServer({ autoComplete: true })
await mock.start()
// Point provider config at localhost — runs full payment flow locally
await mock.stop()
```
